NKJV.1: For the law, having a shadow of the good things to come, and not the very image of the things, can never with these same sacrifices, which they offer continually year by year, make those who approach perfect.
NKJV.2: For then would they not have ceased to be offered? For the worshipers, once purified, would have had no more consciousness of sins.
NKJV.3: But in those sacrifices there is a reminder of sins every year.
NKJV.4: For it is not possible that the blood of bulls and goats could take away sins.
NKJV.5: Therefore, when He came into the world, He said:  “Sacrifice and offering You did not desire, But a body You have prepared for Me.
NKJV.6: In burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin You had no pleasure.
NKJV.7: Then I said, ‘Behold, I have come— In the volume of the book it is written of Me— To do Your will, O God.’ ”
NKJV.8: Previously saying, “Sacrifice and offering, burnt offerings, and offerings for sin You did not desire, nor had pleasure in them” (which are offered according to the law),
NKJV.9: then He said, “Behold, I have come to do Your will, O God.” He takes away the first that He may establish the second.
NKJV.10: By that will we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
NKJV.11: And every priest stands ministering daily and offering repeatedly the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins.
NKJV.12: But this Man, after He had offered one sacrifice for sins forever, sat down at the right hand of God,
NKJV.13: from that time waiting till His enemies are made His footstool.
NKJV.14: For by one offering He has perfected forever those who are being sanctified.
NKJV.15: But the Holy Spirit also witnesses to us; for after He had said before,
NKJV.16: “This is the covenant that I will make with them after those days, says the LORD: I will put My laws into their hearts, and in their minds I will write them,”
NKJV.17: then He adds, “Their sins and their lawless deeds I will remember no more.”
NKJV.18: Now where there is remission of these, there is no longer an offering for sin.
NKJV.19: Therefore, brethren, having boldness to enter the Holiest by the blood of Jesus,
NKJV.20: by a new and living way which He consecrated for us, through the veil, that is, His flesh,
NKJV.21: and having a High Priest over the house of God,
NKJV.22: let us draw near with a true heart in full assurance of faith, having our hearts sprinkled from an evil conscience and our bodies washed with pure water.
NKJV.23: Let us hold fast the confession of our hope without wavering, for He who promised is faithful.
NKJV.24: And let us consider one another in order to stir up love and good works,
NKJV.25: not forsaking the assembling of ourselves together, as is the manner of some, but exhorting one another, and so much the more as you see the Day approaching.
NKJV.26: For if we sin willfully after we have received the knowledge of the truth, there no longer remains a sacrifice for sins,
NKJV.27: but a certain fearful expectation of judgment, and fiery indignation which will devour the adversaries.
NKJV.28: Anyone who has rejected Moses’ law dies without mercy on the testimony of two or three witnesses.
NKJV.29: Of how much worse punishment, do you suppose, will he be thought worthy who has trampled the Son of God underfoot, counted the blood of the covenant by which he was sanctified a common thing, and insulted the Spirit of grace?
NKJV.30: For we know Him who said, “Vengeance is Mine, I will repay,” says the Lord. And again, “The LORD will judge His people.”
NKJV.31: It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
NKJV.32: But recall the former days in which, after you were illuminated, you endured a great struggle with sufferings:
NKJV.33: partly while you were made a spectacle both by reproaches and tribulations, and partly while you became companions of those who were so treated;
NKJV.34: for you had compassion on me in my chains, and joyfully accepted the plundering of your goods, knowing that you have a better and an enduring possession for yourselves in heaven.
NKJV.35: Therefore do not cast away your confidence, which has great reward.
NKJV.36: For you have need of endurance, so that after you have done the will of God, you may receive the promise:
NKJV.37: “For yet a little while, And He who is coming will come and will not tarry.
NKJV.38: Now the just shall live by faith; But if anyone draws back, My soul has no pleasure in him.”
NKJV.39: But we are not of those who draw back to perdition, but of those who believe to the saving of the soul.

ESV.1: For since the law has but a shadow of the good things to come instead of the true form of these realities, it can never, by the same sacrifices that are continually offered every year, make perfect those who draw near.
ESV.2: Otherwise, would they not have ceased to be offered, since the worshipers, having once been cleansed, would no longer have any consciousness of sins?
ESV.3: But in these sacrifices there is a reminder of sins every year.
ESV.4: For it is impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
ESV.5: Consequently, when Christ  came into the world, he said, “Sacrifices and offerings you have not desired, but a body have you prepared for me;
ESV.6: in burnt offerings and sin offerings you have taken no pleasure.
ESV.7: Then I said, ‘Behold, I have come to do your will, O God, as it is written of me in the scroll of the book.’”
ESV.8: When he said above, “You have neither desired nor taken pleasure in sacrifices and offerings and burnt offerings and sin offerings” (these are offered according to the law),
ESV.9: then he added, “Behold, I have come to do your will.” He does away with the first in order to establish the second.
ESV.10: And by that will we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
ESV.11: And every priest stands daily at his service, offering repeatedly the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins.
ESV.12: But when Christ  had offered for all time a single sacrifice for sins, he sat down at the right hand of God,
ESV.13: waiting from that time until his enemies should be made a footstool for his feet.
ESV.14: For by a single offering he has perfected for all time those who are being sanctified.
ESV.15: And the Holy Spirit also bears witness to us; for after saying,
ESV.16: “This is the covenant that I will make with them after those days, declares the Lord: I will put my laws on their hearts, and write them on their minds,”
ESV.17: then he adds, “I will remember their sins and their lawless deeds no more.”
ESV.18: Where there is forgiveness of these, there is no longer any offering for sin.
ESV.19: Therefore, brothers,  since we have confidence to enter the holy places by the blood of Jesus,
ESV.20: by the new and living way that he opened for us through the curtain, that is, through his flesh,
ESV.21: and since we have a great priest over the house of God,
ESV.22: let us draw near with a true heart in full assurance of faith, with our hearts sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies washed with pure water.
ESV.23: Let us hold fast the confession of our hope without wavering, for he who promised is faithful.
ESV.24: And let us consider how to stir up one another to love and good works,
ESV.25: not neglecting to meet together, as is the habit of some, but encouraging one another, and all the more as you see the Day drawing near.
ESV.26: For if we go on sinning deliberately after receiving the knowledge of the truth, there no longer remains a sacrifice for sins,
ESV.27: but a fearful expectation of judgment, and a fury of fire that will consume the adversaries.
ESV.28: Anyone who has set aside the law of Moses dies without mercy on the evidence of two or three witnesses.
ESV.29: How much worse punishment, do you think, will be deserved by the one who has trampled underfoot the Son of God, and has profaned the blood of the covenant by which he was sanctified, and has outraged the Spirit of grace?
ESV.30: For we know him who said, “Vengeance is mine; I will repay.” And again, “The Lord will judge his people.”
ESV.31: It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
ESV.32: But recall the former days when, after you were enlightened, you endured a hard struggle with sufferings,
ESV.33: sometimes being publicly exposed to reproach and affliction, and sometimes being partners with those so treated.
ESV.34: For you had compassion on those in prison, and you joyfully accepted the plundering of your property, since you knew that you yourselves had a better possession and an abiding one.
ESV.35: Therefore do not throw away your confidence, which has a great reward.
ESV.36: For you have need of endurance, so that when you have done the will of God you may receive what is promised.
ESV.37: For, “Yet a little while, and the coming one will come and will not delay;
ESV.38: but my righteous one shall live by faith, and if he shrinks back, my soul has no pleasure in him.”
ESV.39: But we are not of those who shrink back and are destroyed, but of those who have faith and preserve their souls.

NASB.1: For the Law, since it has [only] a shadow of the good things to come [and] not the very form of things, can never, by the same sacrifices which they offer continually year by year, make perfect those who draw near.
NASB.2: Otherwise, would they not have ceased to be offered, because the worshipers, having once been cleansed, would no longer have had consciousness of sins?
NASB.3: But in those [sacrifices] there is a reminder of sins year by year.
NASB.4: For it is impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
NASB.5: Therefore, when He comes into the world, He says, 'SACRIFICE AND OFFERING YOU HAVE NOT DESIRED, BUT A BODY YOU HAVE PREPARED FOR ME;
NASB.6: IN WHOLE BURNT OFFERINGS AND [sacrifices] FOR SIN YOU HAVE TAKEN NO PLEASURE.
NASB.7: 'THEN I SAID, 'BEHOLD, I HAVE COME (IN THE SCROLL OF THE BOOK IT IS WRITTEN OF ME) TO DO YOUR WILL, O GOD.''
NASB.8: After saying above, 'SACRIFICES AND OFFERINGS AND WHOLE BURNT OFFERINGS AND [sacrifices] FOR SIN YOU HAVE NOT DESIRED, NOR HAVE YOU TAKEN PLEASURE [in them]' (which are offered according to the Law),
NASB.9: then He said, 'BEHOLD, I HAVE COME TO DO YOUR WILL.' He takes away the first in order to establish the second.
NASB.10: By this will we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
NASB.11: Every priest stands daily ministering and offering time after time the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins;
NASB.12: but He, having offered one sacrifice for sins for all time, SAT DOWN AT THE RIGHT HAND OF GOD,
NASB.13: waiting from that time onward UNTIL HIS ENEMIES BE MADE A FOOTSTOOL FOR HIS FEET.
NASB.14: For by one offering He has perfected for all time those who are sanctified.
NASB.15: And the Holy Spirit also testifies to us; for after saying,
NASB.16: 'THIS IS THE COVENANT THAT I WILL MAKE WITH THEM AFTER THOSE DAYS, SAYS THE LORD: I WILL PUT MY LAWS UPON THEIR HEART, AND ON THEIR MIND I WILL WRITE THEM,' [He then says],
NASB.17: 'AND THEIR SINS AND THEIR LAWLESS DEEDS I WILL REMEMBER NO MORE.'
NASB.18: Now where there is forgiveness of these things, there is no longer [any] offering for sin.
NASB.19: Therefore, brethren, since we have confidence to enter the holy place by the blood of Jesus,
NASB.20: by a new and living way which He inaugurated for us through the veil, that is, His flesh,
NASB.21: and since [we have] a great priest over the house of God,
NASB.22: let us draw near with a sincere heart in full assurance of faith, having our hearts sprinkled [clean] from an evil conscience and our bodies washed with pure water.
NASB.23: Let us hold fast the confession of our hope without wavering, for He who promised is faithful;
NASB.24: and let us consider how to stimulate one another to love and good deeds,
NASB.25: not forsaking our own assembling together, as is the habit of some, but encouraging [one another]; and all the more as you see the day drawing near.
NASB.26: For if we go on sinning willfully after receiving the knowledge of the truth, there no longer remains a sacrifice for sins,
NASB.27: but a terrifying expectation of judgment and THE FURY OF A FIRE WHICH WILL CONSUME THE ADVERSARIES.
NASB.28: Anyone who has set aside the Law of Moses dies without mercy on [the testimony of] two or three witnesses.
NASB.29: How much severer punishment do you think he will deserve who has trampled under foot the Son of God, and has regarded as unclean the blood of the covenant by which he was sanctified, and has insulted the Spirit of grace?
NASB.30: For we know Him who said, 'VENGEANCE IS MINE, I WILL REPAY.' And again, 'THE LORD WILL JUDGE HIS PEOPLE.'
NASB.31: It is a terrifying thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
NASB.32: But remember the former days, when, after being enlightened, you endured a great conflict of sufferings,
NASB.33: partly by being made a public spectacle through reproaches and tribulations, and partly by becoming sharers with those who were so treated.
NASB.34: For you showed sympathy to the prisoners and accepted joyfully the seizure of your property, knowing that you have for yourselves a better possession and a lasting one.
NASB.35: Therefore, do not throw away your confidence, which has a great reward.
NASB.36: For you have need of endurance, so that when you have done the will of God, you may receive what was promised.
NASB.37: FOR YET IN A VERY LITTLE WHILE, HE WHO IS COMING WILL COME, AND WILL NOT DELAY.
NASB.38: BUT MY RIGHTEOUS ONE SHALL LIVE BY FAITH; AND IF HE SHRINKS BACK, MY SOUL HAS NO PLEASURE IN HIM.
NASB.39: But we are not of those who shrink back to destruction, but of those who have faith to the preserving of the soul.

NLT.1: The old system under the law of Moses was only a shadow, a dim preview of the good things to come, not the good things themselves. The sacrifices under that system were repeated again and again, year after year, but they were never able to provide perfect cleansing for those who came to worship.
NLT.2: If they could have provided perfect cleansing, the sacrifices would have stopped, for the worshipers would have been purified once for all time, and their feelings of guilt would have disappeared.
NLT.3: But instead, those sacrifices actually reminded them of their sins year after year.
NLT.4: For it is not possible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
NLT.5:
That is why, when Christ came into the world, he said to God, “You did not want animal sacrifices or sin offerings.
But you have given me a body to offer.
NLT.6:
You were not pleased with burnt offerings
or other offerings for sin.
NLT.7:
Then I said, ‘Look, I have come to do your will, O God —
as is written about me in the Scriptures.’”
NLT.8: First, Christ said, “You did not want animal sacrifices or sin offerings or burnt offerings or other offerings for sin, nor were you pleased with them” (though they are required by the law of Moses).
NLT.9: Then he said, “Look, I have come to do your will.” He cancels the first covenant in order to put the second into effect.
NLT.10: For God’s will was for us to be made holy by the sacrifice of the body of Jesus Christ, once for all time.
NLT.11: Under the old covenant, the priest stands and ministers before the altar day after day, offering the same sacrifices again and again, which can never take away sins.
NLT.12: But our High Priest offered himself to God as a single sacrifice for sins, good for all time. Then he sat down in the place of honor at God’s right hand.
NLT.13: There he waits until his enemies are humbled and made a footstool under his feet.
NLT.14: For by that one offering he forever made perfect those who are being made holy.
NLT.15: And the Holy Spirit also testifies that this is so. For he says,
NLT.16:
“This is the new covenant I will make
with my people on that day, says the LORD:
I will put my laws in their hearts,
and I will write them on their minds.”
NLT.17:
Then he says, “I will never again remember
their sins and lawless deeds.”
NLT.18: And when sins have been forgiven, there is no need to offer any more sacrifices.
NLT.19: And so, dear brothers and sisters, we can boldly enter heaven’s Most Holy Place because of the blood of Jesus.
NLT.20: By his death, Jesus opened a new and life-giving way through the curtain into the Most Holy Place.
NLT.21: And since we have a great High Priest who rules over God’s house,
NLT.22: let us go right into the presence of God with sincere hearts fully trusting him. For our guilty consciences have been sprinkled with Christ’s blood to make us clean, and our bodies have been washed with pure water.
NLT.23: Let us hold tightly without wavering to the hope we affirm, for God can be trusted to keep his promise.
NLT.24: Let us think of ways to motivate one another to acts of love and good works.
NLT.25: And let us not neglect our meeting together, as some people do, but encourage one another, especially now that the day of his return is drawing near.
NLT.26: Dear friends, if we deliberately continue sinning after we have received knowledge of the truth, there is no longer any sacrifice that will cover these sins.
NLT.27: There is only the terrible expectation of God’s judgment and the raging fire that will consume his enemies.
NLT.28: For anyone who refused to obey the law of Moses was put to death without mercy on the testimony of two or three witnesses.
NLT.29: Just think how much worse the punishment will be for those who have trampled on the Son of God, and have treated the blood of the covenant, which made us holy, as if it were common and unholy, and have insulted and disdained the Holy Spirit who brings God’s mercy to us.
NLT.30:
For we know the one who said, “I will take revenge.
I will pay them back.”
He also said, “The LORD will judge his own people.”
NLT.31: It is a terrible thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
NLT.32: Think back on those early days when you first learned about Christ. Remember how you remained faithful even though it meant terrible suffering.
NLT.33: Sometimes you were exposed to public ridicule and were beaten, and sometimes you helped others who were suffering the same things.
NLT.34: You suffered along with those who were thrown into jail, and when all you owned was taken from you, you accepted it with joy. You knew there were better things waiting for you that will last forever.
NLT.35: So do not throw away this confident trust in the Lord. Remember the great reward it brings you!
NLT.36: Patient endurance is what you need now, so that you will continue to do God’s will. Then you will receive all that he has promised.
NLT.37:
“For in just a little while,
the Coming One will come and not delay.
NLT.38:
And my righteous ones will live by faith.
But I will take no pleasure in anyone who turns away.”
NLT.39: But we are not like those who turn away from God to their own destruction. We are the faithful ones, whose souls will be saved.

WEB.1: For the law, having a shadow of the good to come, not the very image of the things, can never with the same sacrifices year by year, which they offer continually, make perfect those who draw near.
WEB.2: Or else wouldn’t they have ceased to be offered, because the worshipers, having been once cleansed, would have had no more consciousness of sins?
WEB.3: But in those sacrifices there is a yearly reminder of sins.
WEB.4: For it is impossible that the blood of bulls and goats should take away sins.
WEB.5: Therefore when he comes into the world, he says, “Sacrifice and offering you didn’t desire, but you prepared a body for me.
WEB.6: You had no pleasure in whole burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin.
WEB.7: Then I said, ‘Behold, I have come (in the scroll of the book it is written of me) to do your will, O God.’”
WEB.8: Previously saying, “Sacrifices and offerings and whole burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin you didn’t desire, neither had pleasure in them” (those which are offered according to the law),
WEB.9: then he has said, “Behold, I have come to do your will.” He takes away the first, that he may establish the second,
WEB.10: by which will we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
WEB.11: Every priest indeed stands day by day serving and often offering the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins,
WEB.12: but he, when he had offered one sacrifice for sins forever, sat down on the right hand of God;
WEB.13: from that time waiting until his enemies are made the footstool of his feet.
WEB.14: For by one offering he has perfected forever those who are being sanctified.
WEB.15: The Holy Spirit also testifies to us, for after saying,
WEB.16: “This is the covenant that I will make with them: ‘After those days,’ says the Lord, ‘I will put my laws on their heart, I will also write them on their mind;’”   then he says,
WEB.17: “I will remember their sins and their iniquities no more.”
WEB.18: Now where remission of these is, there is no more offering for sin.
WEB.19: Having therefore, brothers, boldness to enter into the holy place by the blood of Jesus,
WEB.20: by the way which he dedicated for us, a new and living way, through the veil, that is to say, his flesh;
WEB.21: and having a great priest over God’s house,
WEB.22: let’s draw near with a true heart in fullness of faith, having our hearts sprinkled from an evil conscience, and having our body washed with pure water,
WEB.23: let us hold fast the confession of our hope without wavering; for he who promised is faithful.
WEB.24: Let us consider how to provoke one another to love and good works,
WEB.25: not forsaking our own assembling together, as the custom of some is, but exhorting one another; and so much the more, as you see the Day approaching.
WEB.26: For if we sin willfully after we have received the knowledge of the truth, there remains no more a sacrifice for sins,
WEB.27: but a certain fearful expectation of judgment, and a fierceness of fire which will devour the adversaries.
WEB.28: A man who disregards Moses’ law dies without compassion on the word of two or three witnesses.
WEB.29: How much worse punishment do you think he will be judged worthy of who has trodden under foot the Son of God, and has counted the blood of the covenant with which he was sanctified an unholy thing, and has insulted the Spirit of grace?
WEB.30: For we know him who said, “Vengeance belongs to me,” says the Lord, “I will repay.”  Again, “The Lord will judge his people.”
WEB.31: It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
WEB.32: But remember the former days, in which, after you were enlightened, you endured a great struggle with sufferings;
WEB.33: partly, being exposed to both reproaches and oppressions; and partly, becoming partakers with those who were treated so.
WEB.34: For you both had compassion on me in my chains, and joyfully accepted the plundering of your possessions, knowing that you have for yourselves a better possession and an enduring one in the heavens.
WEB.35: Therefore don’t throw away your boldness, which has a great reward.
WEB.36: For you need endurance so that, having done the will of God, you may receive the promise.
WEB.37: “In a very little while, he who comes will come, and will not wait.
WEB.38: But the righteous will live by faith. If he shrinks back, my soul has no pleasure in him.”
WEB.39: But we are not of those who shrink back to destruction, but of those who have faith to the saving of the soul.

CSB17.1: Since the law has only a shadow of the good things <sup>ⓘ</sup> to come, and not the reality itself of those things, it can never perfect the worshipers by the same sacrifices they continually offer year after year.
CSB17.2: Otherwise, wouldn’t they have stopped being offered, since the worshipers, purified once and for all, would no longer have any consciousness of sins?
CSB17.3: But in the sacrifices there is a reminder of sins year after year.
CSB17.4: For it is impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
CSB17.5:
Therefore, as he was coming into the world, he said: You did not desire sacrifice and offering,
but you prepared a body for me.
CSB17.6:
You did not delight
in whole burnt offerings and sin offerings.
CSB17.7:
Then I said, “See —
it is written about me
in the scroll —
I have come to do your will, O God.” [1] ⓙ
CSB17.8: After he says above, You did not desire or delight in sacrifices and offerings, whole burnt offerings and sin offerings (which are offered according to the law ⓚ),
CSB17.9: he then says, See, I have come to do your will. [2] He takes away the first to establish the second.
CSB17.10: By this will, we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all time. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.11: Every priest stands day after day ministering and offering the same sacrifices time after time, which can never take away sins. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.12: But this man, after offering one sacrifice for sins forever, sat down at the right hand of God. <sup>[3]</sup> <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.13: He is now waiting until his enemies are made his footstool. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.14: For by one offering he has perfected forever those who are sanctified.
CSB17.15: The Holy Spirit also testifies to us about this. For after he says:
CSB17.16:
This is the covenant I will make with them
after those days,
the Lord says, I will put my laws on their hearts
and write them on their minds,
CSB17.17: andI will never again remember their sins and their lawless acts. [4] ⓑ
CSB17.18: Now where there is forgiveness of these, there is no longer an offering for sin.
CSB17.19: Therefore, brothers and sisters, since we have boldness to enter the sanctuary through the blood of Jesus <sup>ⓒ</sup>  —
CSB17.20: he has inaugurated <sup>[5]</sup> for us a new and living way through the curtain (that is, through his flesh <sup>ⓓ</sup>) —
CSB17.21: and since we have a great high priest over the house of God, <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.22: let us draw near with a true heart in full assurance of faith, with our hearts sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies washed in pure water. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.23: Let us hold on to the confession of our hope without wavering, since he who promised is faithful. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.24: And let us watch out for one another to provoke love and good works,
CSB17.25: not neglecting to gather together, as some are in the habit of doing, but encouraging each other, <sup>ⓗ</sup> and all the more as you see the day approaching.
CSB17.26: For if we deliberately go on sinning after receiving the knowledge of the truth, there no longer remains a sacrifice for sins, <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.27: but a terrifying expectation of judgment and the fury of a fire about to consume the adversaries. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.28: Anyone who disregarded the law of Moses died without mercy, based on the testimony of two or three witnesses. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.29: How much worse punishment do you think one will deserve who has trampled on the Son of God, who has regarded as profane <sup>[6]</sup> the blood of the covenant by which he was sanctified, and who has insulted the Spirit of grace? <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.30:
For we know the one who has said, Vengeance belongs to me; I will repay, [7] [8]
and again, The Lord will judge his people. ⓜ ⓝ
CSB17.31: It is a terrifying thing to fall into the hands of the living God. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.32: Remember the earlier days when, after you had been enlightened, you endured a hard struggle with sufferings. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.33: Sometimes you were publicly exposed to taunts and afflictions, and at other times you were companions of those who were treated that way. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.34: For you sympathized with the prisoners <sup>[9]</sup> and accepted with joy the confiscation of your possessions, because you know that you yourselves have a better and enduring possession. <sup>[10]</sup> <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.35: So don’t throw away your confidence, which has a great reward. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.36: For you need endurance, so that after you have done God’s will, you may receive what was promised. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.37:
For yet in a very little while,
the Coming One will come and not delay.
CSB17.38:
But my righteous one [11] will live by faith;
and if he draws back,
I have no pleasure [12] in him. [13] ⓖ
CSB17.39: But we are not those who draw back and are destroyed, but those who have faith and are saved.

AMP.1: For since the Law has only a shadow [just a pale representation] of the good things to come—not the very image of those things—it can never, by offering the same sacrifices continually year after year, make perfect those who approach [its altars].
AMP.2: For if it were otherwise, would not these sacrifices have stopped being offered? For the worshipers, having once [for all time] been cleansed, would no longer have a consciousness of sin.
AMP.3: But [as it is] these [continual] sacrifices bring a fresh reminder of sins [to be atoned for] year after year,
AMP.4: for it is impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
AMP.5:
Therefore, when Christ enters into the world, He says,
"Sacrifice and offering You have not desired,
But [instead] You have prepared a body for Me [to offer];
AMP.6: In burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin You have taken no delight.
AMP.7:
"Then I said, 'Behold, I have come
To do Your will, O GOD—
[To fulfill] what is written of Me in the scroll of the book.'"
AMP.8: After saying [in the citation] above, "You have neither desired, nor have You taken delight in sacrifices and offerings and whole burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin" (which are offered according to the Law)
AMP.9: then He said, "Behold, I have come to do Your will." [And so] He does away with the first [covenant as a means of atoning for sin based on animal sacrifices] so that He may inaugurate and establish the second [covenant by means of obedience].
AMP.10: And in accordance with this will [of God] we [who believe in the message of salvation] have been sanctified [that is, set apart as holy for God and His purposes] through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed) once for all.
AMP.11: Every priest stands [at his altar of service] ministering daily, offering the same sacrifices over and over, which are never able to strip away sins [that envelop and cover us];
AMP.12: whereas Christ, having offered the one sacrifice [the all-sufficient sacrifice of Himself] for sins for all time, sat down [signifying the completion of atonement for sin] at the right hand of God [the position of honor],
AMP.13: waiting from that time onward <e>until his enemies are made a footstool for His feet</e>.
AMP.14: For by the one offering He has perfected forever and completely cleansed those who are being sanctified [bringing each believer to spiritual completion and maturity].
AMP.15: And the Holy Spirit also adds His testimony to us [in confirmation of this]; for after having said,
AMP.16:
"This is the covenant that I will make with them
After those days, says the Lord:
I will imprint My laws upon their heart,
And on their mind I will inscribe them [producing an inward change],"
He then says,
AMP.17:
"And their sins and their lawless acts
I will remember no more [no longer holding their sins against them]."
AMP.18: Now where there is [absolute] forgiveness and complete cancellation of the penalty of these things, there is no longer any offering [to be made to atone] for sin.
AMP.19: Therefore, believers, since we have confidence and full freedom to enter the Holy Place [the place where God dwells] by [means of] the blood of Jesus,
AMP.20: by this new and living way which He initiated and opened for us through the veil [as in the Holy of Holies], that is, through His flesh,
AMP.21: and since we have a great and wonderful Priest [Who rules] over the house of God,
AMP.22: let us approach [God] with a true and sincere heart in unqualified assurance of faith, having had our hearts sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies washed with pure water.
AMP.23: Let us seize and hold tightly the confession of our hope without wavering, for He who promised is reliable and trustworthy and faithful [to His word];
AMP.24: and let us consider [thoughtfully] how we may encourage one another to love and to do good deeds,
AMP.25: not forsaking our meeting together [as believers for worship and instruction], as is the habit of some, but encouraging one another; and all the more [faithfully] as you see the day [of Christ's return] approaching.
AMP.26: For if we go on willfully and deliberately sinning after receiving the knowledge of the truth, there no longer remains a sacrifice [to atone] for our sins [that is, no further offering to anticipate],
AMP.27: but a kind of awful and terrifying expectation of [divine] judgment and the fury of a fire and burning wrath which will consume the adversaries [those who put themselves in opposition to God].
AMP.28: Anyone who has ignored and set aside the Law of Moses is put to death without mercy on the testimony of two or three witnesses.
AMP.29: How much greater punishment do you think he will deserve who has rejected and trampled under foot the Son of God, and has considered unclean and common the blood of the covenant that sanctified him, and has insulted the Spirit of grace [who imparts the unmerited favor and blessing of God]?
AMP.30: For we know Him who said, "Vengeance is Mine [retribution and the deliverance of justice rest with Me], I will repay [the wrongdoer]." And again, "The Lord will judge His people."
AMP.31: It is a fearful and terrifying thing to fall into the hands of the living God [incurring His judgment and wrath].
AMP.32: But remember the earlier days, when, after being [spiritually] enlightened, you [patiently] endured a great conflict of sufferings,
AMP.33: sometimes by being made a spectacle, publicly exposed to insults and distress, and sometimes by becoming companions with those who were so treated.
AMP.34: For you showed sympathy and deep concern for those who were imprisoned, and you joyfully accepted the [unjust] seizure of your belongings and the confiscation of your property, conscious of the fact that you have a better possession and a lasting one [prepared for you in heaven].
AMP.35: Do not, therefore, fling away your [fearless] confidence, for it has a glorious and great reward.
AMP.36: For you have need of patient endurance [to bear up under difficult circumstances without compromising], so that when you have carried out the will of God, you may receive and enjoy to the full what is promised.
AMP.37:
For yet in a very little while,
He who is coming will come, and will not delay.
AMP.38:
But My righteous one [the one justified by faith] shall live by faith [respecting man's relationship to God and trusting Him];
And if he draws back [shrinking in fear], My soul has no delight in him.
AMP.39: But our way is not that of those who shrink back to destruction, but [we are] of those who believe [relying on God through faith in Jesus Christ, the Messiah] and by this confident faith preserve the soul.

MSG.1: The old plan was only a hint of the good things in the new plan. Since that old "law plan" wasn't complete in itself, it couldn't complete those who followed it. No matter how many sacrifices were offered year after year, they never added up to a complete solution.
MSG.2: If they had, the worshipers would have gone merrily on their way, no longer dragged down by their sins.
MSG.3: But instead of removing awareness of sin, when those animal sacrifices were repeated over and over they actually heightened awareness and guilt.
MSG.4: The plain fact is that bull and goat blood can't get rid of sin.
MSG.5: That is what is meant by this prophecy, put in the mouth of Christ: You don't want sacrifices and offerings year after year; you've prepared a body for me for a sacrifice.
MSG.6: It's not fragrance and smoke from the altar that whet your appetite.
MSG.7: So I said, "I'm here to do it your way, O God, the way it's described in your Book."
MSG.8: When he said, "You don't want sacrifices and offerings," he was referring to practices according to the old plan.
MSG.9: When he added, "I'm here to do it your way," he set aside the first in order to enact the new plan—
MSG.10: God's way—by which we are made fit for God by the once-for-all sacrifice of Jesus.
MSG.11: Every priest goes to work at the altar each day, offers the same old sacrifices year in, year out, and never makes a dent in the sin problem.
MSG.12: As a priest, Christ made a single sacrifice for sins, and that was it! Then he sat down right beside God
MSG.13: and waited for his enemies to cave in.
MSG.14: It was a perfect sacrifice by a perfect person to perfect some very imperfect people. By that single offering, he did everything that needed to be done for everyone who takes part in the purifying process.
MSG.16: This new plan I'm making with Israel isn't going to be written on paper, isn't going to be chiseled in stone; This time "I'm writing out the plan in them, carving it on the lining of their hearts."
MSG.17: He concludes, I'll forever wipe the slate clean of their sins.
MSG.18: Once sins are taken care of for good, there's no longer any need to offer sacrifices for them.
MSG.19: So, friends, we can now—without hesitation—walk right up to God, into "the Holy Place." Jesus has cleared the way by the blood of his sacrifice, acting as our priest before God. The "curtain" into God's presence is his body.
MSG.22: So let's do it—full of belief, confident that we're presentable inside and out.
MSG.23: Let's keep a firm grip on the promises that keep us going. He always keeps his word.
MSG.24: Let's see how inventive we can be in encouraging love and helping out,
MSG.25: not avoiding worshiping together as some do but spurring each other on, especially as we see the big Day approaching.
MSG.26: If we give up and turn our backs on all we've learned, all we've been given, all the truth we now know, we repudiate Christ's sacrifice
MSG.27: and are left on our own to face the Judgment—and a mighty fierce judgment it will be!
MSG.28: If the penalty for breaking the law of Moses is physical death,
MSG.29: what do you think will happen if you turn on God's Son, spit on the sacrifice that made you whole, and insult this most gracious Spirit?
MSG.30: This is no light matter. God has warned us that he'll hold us to account and make us pay. He was quite explicit: "Vengeance is mine, and I won't overlook a thing," and, "God will judge his people."
MSG.31: Nobody's getting by with anything, believe me.
MSG.32: Remember those early days after you first saw the light? Those were the hard times!
MSG.33: Kicked around in public, targets of every kind of abuse—some days it was you, other days your friends.
MSG.34: If some friends went to prison, you stuck by them. If some enemies broke in and seized your goods, you let them go with a smile, knowing they couldn't touch your real treasure. Nothing they did bothered you, nothing set you back.
MSG.35: So don't throw it all away now. You were sure of yourselves then. It's still a sure thing!
MSG.36: But you need to stick it out, staying with God's plan so you'll be there for the promised completion.
MSG.37: It won't be long now, he's on the way; he'll show up most any minute.
MSG.38: But anyone who is right with me thrives on loyal trust; if he cuts and runs, I won't be very happy.
MSG.39: But we're not quitters who lose out. Oh, no! We'll stay with it and survive, trusting all the way.

NIV.1:
Christ’s Sacrifice Once for All
The law is only a shadow of the good things that are coming—not the realities themselves. For this reason it can never, by the same sacrifices repeated endlessly year after year, make perfect those who draw near to worship.
NIV.2: If it could, would they not have stopped being offered? For the worshipers would have been cleansed once for all, and would no longer have felt guilty for their sins.
NIV.3: But those sacrifices are an annual reminder of sins,
NIV.4: because it is impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
NIV.5:
Therefore, when Christ came into the world, he said:
“Sacrifice and offering you did not desire,
but a body you prepared for me;
NIV.6:
with burnt offerings and sin offerings
you were not pleased.
NIV.7:
Then I said, ‘Here I am—it is written about me in the scroll—
I have come to do your will, O God.’”
NIV.8: First he said, “Sacrifices and offerings, burnt offerings and sin offerings you did not desire, nor were you pleased with them” (although the law required them to be made).
NIV.9: Then he said, “Here I am, I have come to do your will.” He sets aside the first to establish the second.
NIV.10: And by that will, we have been made holy through the sacrifice of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
NIV.11: Day after day every priest stands and performs his religious duties; again and again he offers the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins.
NIV.12: But when this priest had offered for all time one sacrifice for sins, he sat down at the right hand of God.
NIV.13: Since that time he waits for his enemies to be made his footstool,
NIV.14: because by one sacrifice he has made perfect forever those who are being made holy.
NIV.15: The Holy Spirit also testifies to us about this. First he says:
NIV.16:
“This is the covenant I will make with them
after that time, says the Lord.
I will put my laws in their hearts,
and I will write them on their minds.”
NIV.17:
Then he adds:
“Their sins and lawless acts
I will remember no more.”
NIV.18: And where these have been forgiven, there is no longer any sacrifice for sin.
NIV.19:
A Call to Persevere
Therefore, brothers, since we have confidence to enter the Most Holy Place by the blood of Jesus,
NIV.20: by a new and living way opened for us through the curtain, that is, his body,
NIV.21: and since we have a great priest over the house of God,
NIV.22: let us draw near to God with a sincere heart in full assurance of faith, having our hearts sprinkled to cleanse us from a guilty conscience and having our bodies washed with pure water.
NIV.23: Let us hold unswervingly to the hope we profess, for he who promised is faithful.
NIV.24: And let us consider how we may spur one another on toward love and good deeds.
NIV.25: Let us not give up meeting together, as some are in the habit of doing, but let us encourage one another—and all the more as you see the Day approaching.
NIV.26: If we deliberately keep on sinning after we have received the knowledge of the truth, no sacrifice for sins is left,
NIV.27: but only a fearful expectation of judgment and of raging fire that will consume the enemies of God.
NIV.28: Anyone who rejected the law of Moses died without mercy on the testimony of two or three witnesses.
NIV.29: How much more severely do you think a man deserves to be punished who has trampled the Son of God under foot, who has treated as an unholy thing the blood of the covenant that sanctified him, and who has insulted the Spirit of grace?
NIV.30: For we know him who said, “It is mine to avenge; I will repay,” and again, “The Lord will judge his people.”
NIV.31: It is a dreadful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
NIV.32: Remember those earlier days after you had received the light, when you stood your ground in a great contest in the face of suffering.
NIV.33: Sometimes you were publicly exposed to insult and persecution; at other times you stood side by side with those who were so treated.
NIV.34: You sympathized with those in prison and joyfully accepted the confiscation of your property, because you knew that you yourselves had better and lasting possessions.
NIV.35: So do not throw away your confidence; it will be richly rewarded.
NIV.36: You need to persevere so that when you have done the will of God, you will receive what he has promised.
NIV.37:
For in just a very little while,
“He who is coming will come and will not delay.
NIV.38:
But my righteous one will live by faith.
And if he shrinks back,
I will not be pleased with him.”
NIV.39: But we are not of those who shrink back and are destroyed, but of those who believe and are saved.

NRSVCE.1: Since the law has only a shadow of the good things to come and not the true form of these realities, it can never, by the same sacrifices that are continually offered year after year, make perfect those who approach.
NRSVCE.2: Otherwise, would they not have ceased being offered, since the worshipers, cleansed once for all, would no longer have any consciousness of sin?
NRSVCE.3: But in these sacrifices there is a reminder of sin year after year.
NRSVCE.4: For it is impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
NRSVCE.5:
Consequently, when Christ came into the world, he said, “Sacrifices and offerings you have not desired,
but a body you have prepared for me;
NRSVCE.6:
in burnt offerings and sin offerings
you have taken no pleasure.
NRSVCE.7:
Then I said, ‘See, God, I have come to do your will, O God’
(in the scroll of the book it is written of me).”
NRSVCE.8: When he said above, “You have neither desired nor taken pleasure in sacrifices and offerings and burnt offerings and sin offerings” (these are offered according to the law),
NRSVCE.9: then he added, “See, I have come to do your will.” He abolishes the first in order to establish the second.
NRSVCE.10: And it is by God’s will that we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
NRSVCE.11: And every priest stands day after day at his service, offering again and again the same sacrifices that can never take away sins.
NRSVCE.12: But when Christ had offered for all time a single sacrifice for sins, “he sat down at the right hand of God,”
NRSVCE.13: and since then has been waiting “until his enemies would be made a footstool for his feet.”
NRSVCE.14: For by a single offering he has perfected for all time those who are sanctified.
NRSVCE.15: And the Holy Spirit also testifies to us, for after saying,
NRSVCE.16:
“This is the covenant that I will make with them
after those days, says the Lord:
I will put my laws in their hearts,
and I will write them on their minds,”
NRSVCE.17: he also adds, “I will remember their sins and their lawless deeds no more.”
NRSVCE.18: Where there is forgiveness of these, there is no longer any offering for sin.
NRSVCE.19: Therefore, my friends, since we have confidence to enter the sanctuary by the blood of Jesus,
NRSVCE.20: by the new and living way that he opened for us through the curtain (that is, through his flesh),
NRSVCE.21: and since we have a great priest over the house of God,
NRSVCE.22: let us approach with a true heart in full assurance of faith, with our hearts sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies washed with pure water.
NRSVCE.23: Let us hold fast to the confession of our hope without wavering, for he who has promised is faithful.
NRSVCE.24: And let us consider how to provoke one another to love and good deeds,
NRSVCE.25: not neglecting to meet together, as is the habit of some, but encouraging one another, and all the more as you see the Day approaching.
NRSVCE.26: For if we willfully persist in sin after having received the knowledge of the truth, there no longer remains a sacrifice for sins,
NRSVCE.27: but a fearful prospect of judgment, and a fury of fire that will consume the adversaries.
NRSVCE.28: Anyone who has violated the law of Moses dies without mercy “on the testimony of two or three witnesses.”
NRSVCE.29: How much worse punishment do you think will be deserved by those who have spurned the Son of God, profaned the blood of the covenant by which they were sanctified, and outraged the Spirit of grace?
NRSVCE.30: For we know the one who said, “Vengeance is mine, I will repay.” And again, “The Lord will judge his people.”
NRSVCE.31: It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
NRSVCE.32: But recall those earlier days when, after you had been enlightened, you endured a hard struggle with sufferings,
NRSVCE.33: sometimes being publicly exposed to abuse and persecution, and sometimes being partners with those so treated.
NRSVCE.34: For you had compassion for those who were in prison, and you cheerfully accepted the plundering of your possessions, knowing that you yourselves possessed something better and more lasting.
NRSVCE.35: Do not, therefore, abandon that confidence of yours; it brings a great reward.
NRSVCE.36: For you need endurance, so that when you have done the will of God, you may receive what was promised.
NRSVCE.37:
For yet “in a very little while,
the one who is coming will come and will not delay;
NRSVCE.38:
but my righteous one will live by faith.
My soul takes no pleasure in anyone who shrinks back.”
NRSVCE.39: But we are not among those who shrink back and so are lost, but among those who have faith and so are saved.

NET.1: For the law possesses a shadow of the good things to come but not the reality itself, and is therefore completely unable, by the same sacrifices offered continually, year after year, to perfect those who come to worship.
NET.2: For otherwise would they not have ceased to be offered, since the worshipers would have been purified once for all and so have no further consciousness of sin?
NET.3: But in those sacrifices there is a reminder of sins year after year.
NET.4: For the blood of bulls and goats cannot take away sins.
NET.5: So when he came into the world, he said, “Sacrifice and offering you did not desire, but a body you prepared for me.
NET.6: “Whole burnt offerings and sin-offerings you took no delight in.
NET.7: “Then I said, ‘Here I am: I have come — it is written of me in the scroll of the book — to do your will, O God.’”
NET.8: When he says above, “Sacrifices and offerings and whole burnt offerings and sin-offerings you did not desire nor did you take delight in them” (which are offered according to the law),
NET.9: then he says, “Here I am: I have come to do your will.” He does away with the first to establish the second.
NET.10: By his will we have been made holy through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
NET.11: And every priest stands day after day serving and offering the same sacrifices again and again — sacrifices that can never take away sins.
NET.12: But when this priest had offered one sacrifice for sins for all time, he sat down at the right hand of God,
NET.13: where he is now waiting until his enemies are made a footstool for his feet.
NET.14: For by one offering he has perfected for all time those who are made holy.
NET.15: And the Holy Spirit also witnesses to us, for after saying,
NET.16: “This is the covenant that I will establish with them after those days, says the Lord. I will put my laws on their hearts and I will inscribe them on their minds,”
NET.17: then he says, “Their sins and their lawless deeds I will remember no longer.”
NET.18: Now where there is forgiveness of these, there is no longer any offering for sin.
NET.19: Therefore, brothers and sisters, since we have confidence to enter the sanctuary by the blood of Jesus,
NET.20: by the fresh and living way that he inaugurated for us through the curtain, that is, through his flesh,
NET.21: and since we have a great priest over the house of God,
NET.22: let us draw near with a sincere heart in the assurance that faith brings, because we have had our hearts sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies washed in pure water.
NET.23: And let us hold unwaveringly to the hope that we confess, for the one who made the promise is trustworthy.
NET.24: And let us take thought of how to spur one another on to love and good works,
NET.25: not abandoning our own meetings, as some are in the habit of doing, but encouraging each other, and even more so because you see the day drawing near.
NET.26: For if we deliberately keep on sinning after receiving the knowledge of the truth, no further sacrifice for sins is left for us,
NET.27: but only a certain fearful expectation of judgment and a fury of fire that will consume God’s enemies.
NET.28: Someone who rejected the law of Moses was put to death without mercy on the testimony of two or three witnesses.
NET.29: How much greater punishment do you think that person deserves who has contempt for the Son of God, and profanes the blood of the covenant that made him holy, and insults the Spirit of grace?
NET.30: For we know the one who said, “Vengeance is mine, I will repay,” and again, “The Lord will judge his people.”
NET.31: It is a terrifying thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
NET.32: But remember the former days when you endured a harsh conflict of suffering after you were enlightened.
NET.33: At times you were publicly exposed to abuse and afflictions, and at other times you came to share with others who were treated in that way.
NET.34: For in fact you shared the sufferings of those in prison, and you accepted the confiscation of your belongings with joy, because you knew that you certainly had a better and lasting possession.
NET.35: So do not throw away your confidence, because it has great reward.
NET.36: For you need endurance in order to do God’s will and so receive what is promised.
NET.37: For just a little longer and he who is coming will arrive and not delay.
NET.38: But my righteous one will live by faith, and if he shrinks back, I take no pleasure in him.
NET.39: But we are not among those who shrink back and thus perish, but are among those who have faith and preserve their souls.
